page: OVHcloud b2-7 is a general-purpose virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 7.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.5 GB of memory per core, alongside 50 GB of local SSD storage. The network configuration includes a baseline bandwidth of 0 Gbps and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate that the b2-7 instance operates in the lower performance tiers. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 1072.92 is categorized as weak (bottom 25%), while the multi-core score of 2156.87 is poor (bottom 10%). Similarly, Geekbench scores reflect limited processing capabilities, with a single-core score of 805.0 and a multi-core score of 1426.0, both falling in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show weak performance, with read-only small block cached speeds at 178.89 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 19.33 GB/sec.

Given the modest CPU and memory performance, this instance is best suited for low-intensity workloads such as basic static web serving, lightweight database operations, or simple background tasks. The static web serving throughput is measured at 2.84 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations reach 3.11 million operations per second, both ranking in the bottom 25%. While the dedicated CPU allocation prevents resource contention, the older Haswell architecture and low benchmark scores represent a tradeoff, positioning this instance as a budget-friendly option for non-critical, low-resource utility services rather than high-throughput production environments.
description: OVHcloud b2-7 is a general-purpose virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 2 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Core (Haswell) processor running at 2.0 GHz, 7.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local SSD storage. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers, with a single-core Geekbench score of 805.0 and a multi-core score of 1426.0, both in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached large block writes at 19.33 GB/sec. Given these performance constraints, the b2-7 is best suited for low-demand workloads such as static web serving, lightweight database operations, and basic utility tasks. It offers a cost-effective entry point for non-production environments where dedicated CPU allocation is required but high computational throughput is not critical.
